title: Comparationes
summary: Bigger, smaller, as good as — comparing things.

teach.target: Le can es plus grande que le catto.
teach.roman: le can es plus grande que le catto
teach.english: The dog is bigger than the cat.
teach.note: plus ... que = more ... than. minus ... que = less ... than.
teach.ipa: le kˈan es plˈus ɡrˈande kˈe le kˈato

teach.target: Le catto es plus parve que le can.
teach.roman: le catto es plus parve que le can
teach.english: The cat is smaller than the dog.
teach.ipa: le kˈato es plˈus pˈarve kˈe le kˈan

grammar.title: Comparing things
grammar.body:
**plus ... que** = more ... than: Marco es plus alte que Anna (Marco is taller than Anna).
**minus ... que** = less ... than: Le catto es minus grande que le can.
**tanto ... como** = as ... as: Illa es tanto felice como ille (she's as happy as he is).
Adjectives stay invariable in comparisons too — no special comparative form to memorize.

teach.target: Mi soror es tanto alte como mi fratre.
teach.roman: mi soror es tanto alte como mi fratre
teach.english: My sister is as tall as my brother.
teach.ipa: mi sorˈor es tˈanto ˈalte kˈomo mi frˈatre

teach.target: Iste caffe es bon, ma iste pan es mal.
teach.roman: iste caffe es bon ma iste pan es mal
teach.english: This coffee is good, but this bread is bad.
teach.ipa: ˈiste kˈafe es bˈon mˈa ˈiste pˈan es mˈal

canDo: Say something is as good as another
